info.name: ParadeDB SQL Interface (No REST API)
info.description: ParadeDB has no REST/HTTP API. It is a PostgreSQL extension stack (pg_search, pg_analytics) consumed as SQL over the PostgreSQL wire protocol (TCP 5432). Postman cannot issue Postgres-protocol queries; the items below are documentation-only stubs recording representative SQL statements, not HTTP requests.

item.name: Full-Text Search (pg_search)
item.description: BM25 full-text search via SQL over the Postgres wire protocol.

request.description: Not an HTTP request. Run as SQL: CREATE EXTENSION pg_search;
Not an HTTP request. Run as SQL: CREATE INDEX search_idx ON mock_items USING bm25 (id, description) WITH (key_field='id');
Not an HTTP request. Run as SQL: SELECT id, paradedb.score(id) FROM mock_items WHERE description @@@ 'keyboard' ORDER BY paradedb.score(id) DESC;

item.name: Analytics (pg_analytics)
item.description: Columnar/OLAP analytics via standard SQL aggregates over the Postgres wire protocol.

request.description: Not an HTTP request. Run as SQL: SELECT category, COUNT(*), AVG(rating) FROM mock_items GROUP BY category;
